mentions.note: 12x earnings (cheapest of banks); Discover acquisition adds heft in credit cards; Discover network can extend reach and drive buybacks. Rate cuts likely under Trump admin. Massive upside.
Bank benefiting from loosening regulations and M&A/equity issuance boom; valuations still expanding after years of compression.
Hammered by credit card rate cap fears that have now faded; plenty of cash; focus on humongous buyback and Discover card network strategy could be gigantic.

Q4 miss on reserve charges but credit metrics solid (charge-offs down); Brex $5.15B fintech acquisition strategic (take on Amex corporate card). Down 16% from highs at 11x earnings vs Amex 21x — at 16x multiple worth $330. Building full-service financial powerhouse.
Caller asked about Synchrony (SYF); Cramer prefers Capital One—pulled back from $259 to $225, better entry; 'travel trust' beneficiary.
Trading ~9x earnings with minimal private-credit exposure; discussed at club meeting; buy despite horrible chart—good entry level, will continue to own.
Top favorite stock; Discover merger finalized; buyback underway; Trump's 10% cap-rate threat fading; expect strong next 12–24 months.
Down 1.66% but down 80 points from highs; credit stress evident; high-rate payers struggling.
Bank rally participant; benefiting from tame delinquencies and strong consumer demand; higher rate environment helps credit card economics.
